Narrative_Meaning: Sixes signify harmony and adaptation, but they can also indicate hard work or unexpected challenges.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Clock card represents Time and timings, obviously, but a clock is a complex and difficult to construct mechanism, and so the card also represents long, complex tasks and procedures. The appearance of the card may indicate that a deadline is in place, that action must take place within a certain time, or that patience and waiting are the only way forwards.

Significator.Plot: The Clock Plot is generally a plot with a time limit. Deadlines, time-limits, ticking bombing countdowns are common enough examples. Of course, a time limit alone isn't the only thing about a Clock Plot. Clock plots also have lots of moving parts, complex set pieces and multiple sides with many Characters.
Significator.Side: A side marked with the Clock has limited time to achieve their goals, or at least they feel the constraints of time during this Plot. It may indicate that the side is aged and need to recruit new Embodiments, that they are becoming desperate and forced to act during the Plot, or that the time of prophecy has come to pass, and they will now follow their plan to victory.
Significator.Character:
The Clock Significator indicates a Character who is regulated and unforgiving.

They Gain Yin when they hold a grudge, recall an event, or talk about the past.

They Gain Yang when they plan ahead, talk about the future, or make a schedule.

They Gain Chi when they are on time, living in the moment, or check the time.

They also gain +1 Success Level on any action to do with timing, punctuality or precision.
Significator.Scene: The Clock Scene is any Scene that time is important in. It may be a specific temporal event (such as the start of a world war, or a presidential assassination) or just a timed event (such as a race or sport), it can also be used to imply extra time (perhaps outside of normal time like Nevertime). A Yarn-Teller can play a Clock card to impose a time-limit or negate it. They might use it to create some free time to recover, or limit how long a target may be able to access their Lores or Descendants.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Hitches, the bigger the better.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The story hooks the character with a countdown situation. There is limited time until something major happens. How major will vary on the Stakes, with low stakes being responding to a school reunion RSVP, and High Stakes being ambulances racing through traffic to get to a accident scene.
Yarn.Gain: Time (some sense of an extension in a deadline or additional life to live)
Yarn.Fray_Description: Precise, meticulous planning, preparation and timing is required for execution of clockwork, whether its building a clock, or robbing a bank, clockwork is a Complex Procedural and Motional Ordeal that can be of any Stakes. Timing is a natural part of the Clockwork Fray and you can model that with limited Phases per Stage.
Yarn.Snag_Description: The Fray may be affected by a Time Limit, this is usually applied as a Limit on the total number of Phases that the Ordeal must be completed in (or at least that a certain Stage must be completed by), note that this is in addition to any Phases per Stage Limit that may already be in place because of the Stakes). If a Time Limit is inappropriate for some reason, then the situation must be complicated by History, with past choices limiting future choices.

NPC.Description: The Historian or Chronicler investigates some events, and then turns those investigations into stories that they write and sell.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PCs are not doing well, they need to rest badly, in fact they may be ill from festering wounds, the way everything keeps spinning... They come around in hospital, or at least a bed somewhere, being nursed back to health, they may still be injured, but their wounds have been cleaned and bandaged, and perhaps some antibiotic poultices have been applied, if you are lucky.
Yarn.History_Description: The Clock indicates that the past of the current situation is a complex, complicated thing, often with many moving parts.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Clock indicates that the current situation is a limited time deal, there is a ticking time bomb or a time limit of some sort on the current situation before it changes, usually to something worse.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Clock indicates that the current situation will be best solved by time. This can sometimes mean that waiting patiently, or hiding in a hole is the way to solve the problem, and just occasionally time travel is the best solution.

Revelations.About_Description: The information revealed is about the External Conflict Embodiment. This is a side that is considered outside of the normal Conflict, this could be aliens, or more likely, the next town over.
Revelations.Info_Description: Information about an event that is happening, will happen, or already did, take place.
Revelations.Vector_Description: The Character will be given evidence that points towards the information, but they will have to put the pieces together themselves (and probably get it wrong at least once).
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is about ways, ways things can be done, ways things can go, and ways into. and out of, town without being noticed by the guard.
Revelations.Detail_Description: The detail is a truism, or at least an aphorism, or saying, that strongly applies to the situation.

Obstacle.Description: The way is blocked by some mechanism (such as a raising or rotating bridge, crane or vehicle), often used to create complex mechanical traps, that usually have some timer and reset mechanism, such as crushing walls, swinging blades, spinning chains. Such mechanisms usually have some hidden reset or control panel.

Tarot.Crossed: Target may automatically Stalemate any Test (Scoring exactly the Difficulty). Automatically pay a Stage / Obstacle cost in an Ordeal
Tarot.Normal: Automatically gives target 1 Unsoakable Distract Wound and adds +1 Success Level and +1 Failure Level to their next action.

Ordeal.Stage_Description: There's a lot of potential dangers in the darkness, ranging from the occasional Lego brick, or upturned plug, or nail, to holes and of course, monsters.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: The hover-board gangs boards roared as they engaged their plasma burners, and they raced for us, as I took the ornithopter over a grav-transport and onto the north-bound express level.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Tide of Battle is difficult to read for both sides, and neither side will have any advantage. Visibilty is restricted by fog, smoke, darkness or similar such that when two combatants threaten each other they are already at engaged distances. Disengagement Actions have two automatic Success Levels that may cancel a Failure Level.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
The Fight is a seriously dangerous one. High or Extreme Stakes against a stream of Cast Monsters (with at least 3 Monster Facets).
  Facet    Monster  
Gossamer ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Wiry — The Monster is immensely physically powerful, although often its form may not reveal it. It often demonstrates supernatural power as well as strength and toughness. A small, strong Goblin, a robot that looks like a little girl, a cybernetically enhanced rat, or even a killer doll (when not an Object Monster) are all examples of a Gossamer Monster. The Wiry Monster can add Gossamer Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever it is underestimated, cared for, or demonstrates its strength. A Wiry monster may add their current Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any action with their strength or toughness. Wiries must spend cards from their Monster Pool as though it was a Prepared Defence to ignore damage.
Inertia ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Unstoppable — The Monster is inexorable, it does not stop and cannot be killed by any normal means. They start play with a Monster Pool of Inertia Score cards. Unstoppable Monsters can add Inertia Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cross a threshold, pass a barrier, or are forced to stop or turn around. Unstoppable Monsters can ignore a number of Wounds equal to the number of cards in their Monster Pool. Wounds stacked beyond the Monster Pool affect them as normal. Inexorable Monsters can also ignore all Hitches with a Boon less than their Monster Pool Size. Whenever an Unstoppable Monster is damaged beyond this limit, it must Discard the Highest Card in the Monster Pool, it may immediately also Discard any Wounds lower than discarded Monster Card, any remaining Wounds are then discarded on a one Monster Pool card per Wound basis. However, while discarding these wounds and cards the Monster must remain still (playing dead) until a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Number of Monster Pool cards Discarded (the initial card is instant, the next discard takes an action, then a second takes a few seconds, the third Wound takes an Ordeal Round, 25 Wounds would take a week). Examples of the Inertial Monster type include the Hungry Dead (Zombies) and most robotic assassins (especially ones from the future).
Key ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Hunter — The Monster is a hunter or tracker, it should favour harrying attacks and hit and run tactics. Typically such a Monster will vanish or flee if it is wounded, only to reappear when healed. The hunter may be fooled by false trails, but never for long. Hunters may add Key Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they spot a track, clue, or similar, that indicates the subject of their hunt. Hunters receive a bonus to their Score or Pips equal to the number of cards in the Monster Pool on any tracking, attacking or hunting actions if they also spend a card from the Monster Pool on the action. Hunters may convert Mortal and Carnage Wounds that they take into Crippling Wounds instead, at a cost of one card from their Monster Pool. In T13, the archetypal version of this Monster is a Bulmäs Pack member from the Wild Hunt, but any Werewolf or Animal Shifter would probably qualify, as would robot cowboys in theme parks that have gone wrong, and any fantasy world’s Elven ranger.
Miasma ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Mutant — The monster is a mutant or chimerical creature made from parts of others. This monster type is the oldest perhaps, you’ll find examples throughout the ancient world, particularly in Greek myth. The Mutant will often be larger, and may be malformed or unsymmetrical, with odd numbers of limbs, eyes, horns, or even heads. The Chimera will blend multiple animals together to create a new creature that combines features of all the “parent” creatures, preserving the best of all species. Mutant Monsters start with Miasma Score Monster Pool. They may add Miasma Draw cards to the Pool every time they accomplish a specified goal, such as eating a human, consuming a soul, bathing in radiation, or just growing a year older, etc. The Monster Pool can be used as a “Mutation” Annex, which can be used to mimic the abilities of the Mutants “parent” creatures, such as a spider’s ability to climb, or an eagle’s ability to fly. The Mutation Annex has a Boon equal to the Value of the Number of Cards in the Monster Pool, and so draws cards equal to the Monster Pool Size Reduced and plays a number of cards equal to the Miasma Draw. The Mutation Annex is often used as a Size Annex for the Mutant. Where a 5 card Monster Pool would create a Boon 16 Annex, and could grant a Kaiju Mutant the size of a Skyscraper. 1950s B-movie monsters are often of this type, giant ants, lizards, spiders, wasps, and even rabbits have been used. Classically of course, Cyclopses, Centaurs, Minotaurs, Satyrs, Gryphons (or Griffins), Hippogriffs, Dragons, Sphinxes, Manticores and of course the Chimera itself are all examples. You can also find examples in superheroes, especially those created by radioactive arachnids, or those with an X-gene.
Trial ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Killer — The Killer Monster is a deadly Combat specialist of some sort. Killers add Trial Draw cards to their Monster Pool each time they kill. Killers automatically add +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to all attacks, and Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) is automatically added to the Pips of any attack as Penetration. Killers must spend Monster Pool cards whenever they will make a Trick either with other cards being played by the Ordeal Pool, or other cards in the Round Pool or a Style Reserve. Killers can be drug-addled assassins, crazed shooters, or just steel-souled soldiers.
Yonder ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Alien — The Monster is an alien, an outsider. It may just be a metaphor for alienation, or literally be an extra-(or ultra)-terrestrial. Such monsters are usually obvious and inhuman, not just to look at, but in the way they think and behave. They may have apparently supernatural powers and abilities, but often make do with advanced technologies (in the form of additional Descendants). They may add Yonder Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they are observed doing their alien / inhuman / weird actions. They may add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels to any action to do with their alien abilities, technologies, motion or survival (including Dodge, Soak, etc). Alien Monsters must play cards from their Monster Pool to socialise with “non-aliens”. From little grey Eusaiveans, through pod people and pepper-pot shaped engines of hate, all the way to face-hugging space-wasps, there are plenty of aliens.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Notes: Animals smaller than 40 Kilograms, usually eat small mammals and birds. Very common path in human occupied territories.

Stress.Description: Stress and its results can make a person feel like their life is spiralling out of their control. This can result in all sorts of problems as parts of the Character’s life are damaged, discarded, lost, chased away, replaced, or forgotten.
Stress.Rules: Characters who are spiralling are offered a choice. They may take a new Hitch (Binding, Laments, Confined, Corrupted, Offensive or Loss of Sense) with Banes equal to the the Character’s current Stress, or they may increase another Hitch by 1 Bane for Stress equal to the current Hitch Banes, or they may discard parts of their Character such as Proficiencies, Descendants, Allies or even Annexes which will negate Stress equal to the Cost of the lost part in Chi

Trauma.Description: Trauma can have profound effects on a Character, altering how they perceive time and sequences of events. It can cause them to become regimented, adhering to a strict time-table for everything in their life, but it can also adjust a schedule, so that a Character becomes aways early or late.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma alters a Character’s relationship with time and changes it. The Character may now choose how they wish to Relieve 1 Stress which can be: by always being exactly, strictly, on time, by always being significantly early for anything, or by always being exceptionally late for anything.

Age.Description: An Age of Urgency is occurring. During this age a society seems to lose patience, everything becomes hurried, with members pressured by society to be swift or even hasty. This urgency throughout society can appear in the individual as boldness, excitement, enthusiasm, fervor, insistence, persistence, desperation, uneasiness, frustration, weariness or hesitency. Sometimes an Age of Urgency is brought on by a need to complete some endeavour or great work quickly, but more often it is a general acceleration of life creating a lack of patience in society. Human progress can largely be tied to the speed of travel and communications that we have achieved.
Age.Rules: During an Age of Urgency all members of a society will gain Stress equal to how long their task will take as an Extend Duration as Yarn (e.g. any task that takes longer than a second will add at least 1 Stress, longer than a moment, but less than two hours will add 2 Stress. Taking on a year or two year long task will add 10 Stress, etc.
